title: Silky Floral British Nectarine Soup
description: A fragrant, silky soup of poached nectarines with citrus, ginger and coconut cream—ideal as a hangover cure or light lunch.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine sliced nectarines, water, lemon juice, grated ginger, cinnamon stick, and orange zest. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat. Cook for 8 minutes until nectarines are soft and aromatic.
Remove the cinnamon stick. Transfer the mixture to a blender, add honey, and blend until silky smooth. Strain through a fine mesh sieve into a clean bowl for a velvety finish.
Stir in coconut milk and almond milk until fully incorporated. Warm gently over low heat, watching carefully to avoid boiling (heat not above 80°C / 176°F).
Taste and season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to balance the sweetness.
Let the soup cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to chill and allow flavors to meld.
Serve chilled or slightly warmed over a low flame. Garnish each bowl with a sprig of fresh thyme.
If desired, swirl a small amount of lemon verbena or edible flower into the soup for an extra floral note.
Enjoy this silky, floral British nectarine soup as a hangover cure or a refreshing light lunch.
